# domain-monitor

## Install

```bash
composer require rasuvaeff/domain-monitor
```

## Rules

- HTTP services require a PSR-18 client and a PSR-17 request factory.
- Host-only inputs go to host services; HTTP services require `http://` or `https://` URLs.
- `SslCertificateService` reads peer certificates best-effort and does not verify trust.
- `DomainMonitor` orchestrator runs all configured services in one call, reuses the HTTP response for probe + security headers + content check. Services are optional: `null` = check disabled.
- `DomainMonitor::create($client, $factory, $whois?)` wires every check with defaults; `DomainMonitorBuilder` composes granularly. `DomainMonitor implements DomainMonitorInterface`.
- `DomainMonitor` catches exceptions per check; a failed check becomes a `CheckError` (see `getErrors()`/`hasErrors()`), reported as `UNKNOWN`, not silently dropped. Disabled check = `null`.
- Read the report via `getChecks()` (`list<CheckResult>` with `reason`) / `getCheck(CheckName)`; `getStatus()` is the aggregate.
- Thresholds: `ReportThresholds` (opt-in via `DomainMonitorOptions`) makes SSL "expiring soon" and the WHOIS window configurable; `default()` = legacy behaviour.
- Every result DTO is `JsonSerializable`: `json_encode($report)` — dates ISO-8601, enums as values, disabled checks `null`.

## API reference

### Run all checks at once (DomainMonitor orchestrator)

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitor;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitorOptions;

$monitor = new DomainMonitor(
    httpProbe: $probeService,
    ssl: $sslService,
    whois: $whoisService,
    dns: $dnsService,
    port: $portService,
    securityHeaders: $headersService,
    robotsTxt: $robotsService,
    sitemap: $sitemapService,
    content: $contentService,
);

$report = $monitor->check(
    host: 'example.com',
    options: new DomainMonitorOptions(
        port: 443,
        timeoutSeconds: 10.0,
        httpMethod: 'GET',
        expectedOrg: 'Example Inc.',
        requiredText: 'Welcome',
        thresholds: ReportThresholds::strict(),
    ),
);

echo $report->getStatus()->value;
```

Constructor invariant: `SecurityHeadersService` requires `HttpProbeService` (it needs the HTTP response).

### Factory / builder

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitor;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainMonitorBuilder;

$monitor = DomainMonitor::create(httpClient: $client, requestFactory: $factory, whois: $whois);

$monitor = DomainMonitorBuilder::create()
    ->withHttp(client: $client, requestFactory: $factory)
    ->withWhois($whois)
    ->withoutPort()
    ->build();
```

### Read results

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\CheckName;

foreach ($report->getChecks() as $result) {
    echo "{$result->check->value} {$result->status->value} {$result->reason}\n";
}

$ssl = $report->getCheck(name: CheckName::Ssl); // ?CheckResult

if ($report->hasErrors()) {
    foreach ($report->getErrors() as $error) {
        echo "{$error->check->value}: {$error->message}\n"; // CheckError
    }
}

$json = json_encode($report, JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR);
```

### Diff two reports into status transitions

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\ReportComparator;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\TransitionKind;

// Stateless: the app owns the previous snapshot (storage). diff($r, $r) === [].
$diff = (new ReportComparator())->compare(previous: $previousReport, current: $currentReport);

if ($diff->hasChanges()) {
    foreach ($diff->getTransitions() as $t) {
        // StatusTransition: check (CheckName), from (?CheckStatus), to (?CheckStatus), kind (TransitionKind)
        echo "{$t->check->value} {$t->from?->value} -> {$t->to?->value} {$t->kind->value}\n";
    }
    $worst = $diff->worstTransition(); // ?StatusTransition, most severe `to`
}
// diff(): list<StatusTransition>; compare(): ReportDiff wrapper.
// TransitionKind: Appeared (from=null), Disappeared (to=null), Degraded, Recovered,
// Changed (to/from UNKNOWN — severity not comparable). All types are JsonSerializable.
```

### Normalize input

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HostNormalizer;

$normalizer = new HostNormalizer();
$host = $normalizer->normalizeHost(hostOrUrl: 'https://Example.com:8443/path');
$url = $normalizer->normalizeUrl(url: 'https://Example.com/path');
```

### Probe a URL

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeOptions;
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeService;

$result = $probeService->check(
    url: 'https://example.com',
    options: new HttpProbeOptions(
        method: 'GET',
        headers: ['Accept' => 'text/html'],
    ),
);
```

### Check SSL, WHOIS, DNS

```php
$ssl = $sslService->check(host: 'example.com', expectedOrg: 'Example');
$whois = $whoisService->check(host: 'example.com');
$dns = $dnsService->check(host: 'example.com');
```

### Check content, ports, headers, robots, sitemap

```php
$content = $contentService->check(url: 'https://example.com', requiredText: 'Example');
$port = $portService->check(host: 'example.com', port: 443);
$headers = $headersService->check(response: $response);
$robots = $robotsService->check(baseUrl: 'https://example.com');
$sitemap = $sitemapService->check(sitemapUrl: 'https://example.com/sitemap.xml');
```

### Build a report

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\DomainHealthReport;

$report = new DomainHealthReport(
    host: 'example.com',
    probe: $probe,
    ssl: $ssl,
    whois: $whois,
    dns: $dns,
    port: $port,
    content: $content,
    securityHeaders: $headers,
    robotsTxt: $robots,
    sitemap: $sitemap,
);

$status = $report->getStatus(); // ok | warning | critical | unknown
```

### Probe with response reuse

```php
use Rasuvaeff\DomainMonitor\HttpProbeWithResponse;

$probeWithResponse = $probeService->probeWithResponse(url: 'https://example.com');
$probeResult = $probeWithResponse->result;
$response = $probeWithResponse->response;

// Reuse response for security headers and content check
$headers = $headersService->check(response: $response);
$content = $contentService->checkFromResponse(response: $response, requiredText: 'Example');
```
